{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|34|58|36|N|138|46|1|E}}}} |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|qid=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Numazu|Q241037|qid=Q241037|1=Numazu}}{{ill|lt=Heda Village (Shizuoka)|Q5697064|qid=Q5697064|lt=Heda|1=Heda, Shizuoka}}2710 |位置 = {{coord|34|58|36|N|138|46|1|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Ototachibana-hime|Q11487396|qid=Q11487396|lt=Ototachibana-hime-no-Mikoto|1=Tachibanahime-no-Mikoto}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}}, former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|qid=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建 = |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = April 4 |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=Morokuchi Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|34|58|36|N|138|46|1|E}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} }} [[ファイル:Heda torii.jpg|thumb|250px|Torii]] [[ファイル:Fuji (37929951324).jpg|thumb|250px|{{ill|lt=Mihama Cape|Q11489198|qid=Q11489198|1=Cape Mihama}}, torii, and {{ill|lt=Mount Fuji|Q39231|qid=Q39231|1=Mount Fuji}}]] '''Morokuchi Shrine''' (もろくちじんじゃ[https://numazukanko.jp/fc/location/50018 Morokuchi Shrine] - Numazu City Tourism Strategy Division, also read もろぐちじんじゃ[http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4402126 Morokuchi Shrine] - Shizuoka Prefectural Shrine Association) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Heda Village (Shizuoka)|Q5697064|qid=Q5697064|lt=Heda|1=Heda, Shizuoka}}, {{ill|lt=Numazu|Q241037|qid=Q241037|1=Numazu}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|qid=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}. It is located facing east at the tip of {{ill|lt=Mihama Cape|Q11489198|qid=Q11489198|1=Cape Mihama}}. A red torii visible even from the opposite shore stands along the sea in front. It is jointly managed by the priest of {{ill|lt=Heda Shrine|Q92443788|qid=Q92443788|1=Heda Shrine}} in the same Heda district[https://web.archive.org/web/20210920093819/https://ananweb.jp/news/373262/amp/ Hiroyuki Ehara: "I felt immeasurable energy" - two spiritual spots in Numazu] - {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=anan|1=UNKNOWN}} 2021/9/18. === Overview === Founding is unknown. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}} presumed to be one of the two "Kunitama-no-Mikoto Shrines" listed in the {{ill|lt=Naka District, Izu Province section|Q11380552|qid=Q11380552|lt=Naka District section of Izu Province|1=List of Shikinaisha in Izu Province}} in the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|qid=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}'. From the Jinmyōchō description, the original enshrined deities are inferred as "Moroki-hime, Kunitama-hime, and Tachibana-hime," and the present name "Morokuchi" is thought to derive from that "Moroki"Precinct origin board. As a goddess, it was long identified with {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Benten|1=UNKNOWN}} and revered by fishermen. In August {{ill|lt=1879|Q7783|qid=Q7783|1=1879}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|qid=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 12), it was designated village shrine. The current shrine building was rebuilt in April {{ill|lt=1953|Q18585|qid=Q18585|1=1953}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|qid=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 28). === Enshrined deity === * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ototachibana-hime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} === Precinct === * Shrine building * Precinct shrine * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Temizuya|1=UNKNOWN}} * {{ill|lt=Torii|Q234731|qid=Q234731|1=Torii}} (front east side and rear southwest side) === Cultural properties === * Morokuchi Shrine's {{ill|lt=waniguchi|Q11674315|qid=Q11674315|1=Waniguchi}} (Numazu City designated cultural property[https://www.city.numazu.shizuoka.jp/shisei/profile/bunkazai/img/ichiran.pdf List of Numazu City designated cultural properties] - Numazu City) === Festivals === * Grand annual festival: April 4 === Surroundings === * To the south-southeast in front are beaches such as "Kobunegahama" and "Mihama Beach." * On the west side behind the shrine are the {{ill|lt=Numazu City Heda Shipbuilding Museum|Q11554523|qid=Q11554523|lt=Heda Shipbuilding Local History Museum|1=Heda Shipbuilding Museum}} and the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Suruga Bay Deep Sea Aquarium|1=UNKNOWN}}. === Access === * {{ill|lt=Izu-Hakone Railway|Q864568|qid=Q864568|1=Izuhakone Railway}} "{{ill|lt=Shuzenji Station|Q7505799|qid=Q7505799|1=Shuzenji Station}}" then {{ill|lt=Tokai Bus|Q11527778|qid=Q11527778|1=Tokai Bus}} (bound for Heda) ** Get off at "Heda" bus stop, then walk 20 minutes, circling in from the southeast along the coast. === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === * [http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4402126 Morokuchi Shrine] - Shizuoka Prefectural Shrine Association == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Morokuchi Shrine (Q116694076)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Morokuchi Shrine (Q116694076)]][[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izu Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:沼津市の歴史]] [[Category:沼津市の建築物]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izu Province]] [[Category:静岡県の旧村社]]
